content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates consistent, timely, and specific reads throughout. Turn 2 catches the laugh as a tell. Turn 4 names the precise linguistic switch ('doing this to myself' vs 'this keeps happening') — a classic AoA caveat read. Turn 12 names the rush-into-intellectualization signature ('moved pretty fast from winning into the neuroscience') at the exact moment it happens, which produces the conversation's biggest opening. Reads consistently precede questions: the coach names what they noticed, then inquires. The coach also tracks the somatic/energetic shift in turn 8 (following the distance-appearing moment) and stays on the relief rather than letting the client flee it in turn 14. These are specific reads — linguistic caveat, the intellectualization-as-defense, the doorway-hovering — not generic mood labels.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the laugh as signal, names it, inquires underneath — read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the specific linguistic switch between agency and passivity — a precise caveat read."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Names the contradiction in her stance but as content reframe rather than a somatic/energetic read."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good question following the face-shift but doesn't explicitly name what was just noticed."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Follows the stopping-herself moment but doesn't name the self-interruption as a read."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the rush-into-neuroscience defense in real time — the pivotal read of the conversation that lands and opens the client."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds steady, refuses agenda, invites her to stay with the felt state — implicit read that she was about to bolt again."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["looking-for-whats-wrong"],
  "notes": "The intellectualization-as-defense read in turn 12 is the highest-value read and it lands. The coach could have additionally named the disdain/sharpness toward exes as a read, but the reads that did surface were specific, timely, and consequential."
}
